preamble: The elision or deletion of the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] is a common form of lenition, particularly frequent in syllable-final (coda) positions or as a result of simplifying complex consonant clusters in historical sound changes.

examples.note: Frequent deletion of the word-final uvular rhotic in fast speech and certain social registers.
Laryngeal deletion theory (Rasmussen) where uvular fricatives were lost in specific phonotactic environments.
Adaptation where the French uvular rhotic is reinterpreted as a vowel glide or deleted.

sources: Rasmussen, J. E. (1989). Studien zur Morphophonemik der indogermanischen Grundsprache.
